posts.title: The death of the fun project
posts.summary: What are we supposed to live off of after the oil? Making AI B2B SaaSses on our spare time?
posts.content: As (basic) technical competence becomes superfluous, or at least as a code project (by an (incompetent) individual) will never be impressive anymore, because it can be or is vibe coded, how can you stand out, be proud of things you make, and do cool meaningful things? If I build something slightly ambitious, e.g. a platform for backtesting quantitative algorhitms on prediction markets (e.g. polymarket), this won't be as cool or impressive as 7 years ago, because now everyone can do it with the help of AI tols. Then it's suddenly physical things, robotics, production engineering and other stuff AI doesn't have hands for that you have to do to stand out. It removes some of the joy of computers, that it's not as honorable anymore. It's a bit like when black air forces or moncler got ruined by all the gangstrs tending to wear it. Or fur coats by all the prostitutes. Or bouldering by all the crossfitters. In isolation it's still cool, but everyone has associations to something tacky or less honorable - so how cool is it? My question is what to do, which projects, what should I learn more about and interest myself in? Like they say at Mac, i believe two things: I have a hypothesis that metaprojects are a way to go, meaning projects where you create or improve/exploit these AI tools in the best possible way, but they only work in an interim period. Another hypothesis is that you simply have to upscale the projects, meaning that where it used to take a week to put together a crossword solver back in the day, you now have to spend a week building a trading platform for cobalt(?, much more ambitious, I imagine). They both have snags, the former that it has a limited time period (eventually everything will catch up or become too complex for "the regular joe"), and the latter has two snags: it will only accellarate, so the complexity here will increase exponentially, and it gets expensive, in tokens, hosting and other resource usage. Besides there's a limited number of vertically integrated ai-native (r)ecommerce platforms for dog socks that are needed. So we'll see then. AI is here to stay, and this is the worst the agents will ever be.

posts.title: I am scared for my Laif!
posts.summary: Experimenting with a OpenClaw-based agentic system to properly coach me in sports and in general l(a)if (haha) sh leif.
posts.content:
I've been my own coach for a while. Needed to distrubute the load of hacing to deal with me and my training and school. Now, Laif knows me almost as good as I know myself, and I find it scary, fun and helpful.

The name is a play on 'life', 'ai'(it's here to stay) and 'leif olav alnes'. 

### How it works

Built on OpenClaw, which is a open-sourced framwork for personal agentic AI I've been tinkering with. It's based around skills and tools for the agent to call — strava for training data, plugga for study and exam load, weather, calendar, and so on. 
The gbrains part is a memory setup, which with some tweaking worked better for my use case. Standard RAG so he can recall my sessions, exams and life stuff n
### Does it work?

Sorta as they say in the states. It's very expensive, so I am using some of the left over tokens from Dalai. It was too complex for sonnet 4.6, so I pivoted to opus for better (and more expensive) quality. It is not scarily good, but as they say on linkedin: this is the worst they will ever be.

It is cool and a steep learning curve, and I just hope not to go bankrupt due to hundreds of cron jobs and heartbeats each using millions of tokens. Take care.

posts.title: Hi Claude, please make it say "Hello, world"
posts.summary: First post on wwwik.no. Will probably forget to write any more.
posts.content:
This is the first post @ **wwwik.no**.

I built this as a home for my CV, projects, and occasional writing.  I want to take record of things I've worked on and i had a great idea for a domain name.

### What to expect

Mix of:
- Contrary to popular belief: **NO AI SLOP** (it's true) 
- Notes on things I'm building
- Accomplishments in a braggy way 
- Accomplishments in a humble way 
- Thoughts that I write procrastination essays about

Feel free to contact me if you read anything on here.
